question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: All states have very diverse population levels.
options.B: The population gap between Tennessee and South Carolina might exceed that between West Virginia and Florida.
options.C: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.D: Tennessee and South Carolina have similar populations.

explanation: Tennessee (37.4M) and South Carolina (49.4M) have a 12.0M population difference, while West Virginia (29.8M) and Florida (30.8M) have only a 1.0M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Tennessee and South Carolina into the same color bin (30M+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while West Virginia and Florida appear more different due to different color bins.
